name: data-lineage-viz
description: Visualize object dependencies and data lineage from .dacpac files or by importing from SQL Server, Azure SQL, Fabric DW, and Synapse databases

commands.title: Data Lineage: Create AI Output Templates
Data Lineage: Toggle Schema Overview Mode
Data Lineage: Show AI Trace in Graph

dataLineageViz.maxNodes.markdownDescription: Maximum number of database objects to load. The graph rendering is separately capped by `renderLimit`. Values above 1000 are supported for AI/data queries but the GUI will show schema overview or a limit warning.

dataLineageViz.renderLimit.markdownDescription: Maximum rendered nodes before showing a warning instead of the graph. Prevents browser performance issues with very large visualizations. AI tools and BFS operate on the full model regardless of this limit.

dataLineageViz.excludePatterns.markdownDescription: Regex patterns to permanently exclude objects by name at load time (e.g. `_TEST$`, `^staging\\.`). Matched against `schema.object`. Supports `%` wildcard. Requires reloading the data source to take effect. For interactive ad-hoc filtering without reload, use the Exclusion Rules toolbar button.

dataLineageViz.externalRefs.enabled.markdownDescription: Detect external references (OPENROWSET file paths, cross-database 3-part names). When disabled, no virtual external nodes are created. External Tables are unaffected.

dataLineageViz.overview.enabled.markdownDescription: Enable schema overview mode. When enabled and the graph exceeds `overview.threshold` nodes, the initial view shows schema-level bubbles instead of all objects. Set to `false` to always show the full object graph.

dataLineageViz.overview.threshold.markdownDescription: Node count above which schema overview mode auto-activates on initial load. Only applies when `overview.enabled` is `true`.

dataLineageViz.overview.forceOverviewThr.markdownDescription: Node count above which overview mode is forced even after manual toggle (soft guard). Prevents accidentally rendering very large graphs. Must be >= `overview.threshold`.

dataLineageViz.parseRulesFile.markdownDescription: Path to a custom parse rules YAML file. Leave empty for built-in defaults. [Customization guide](https://github.com/ChrisDevRepo/vscode_data_lineage/blob/main/docs/PARSE_RULES.md)

dataLineageViz.dmvQueryTimeout.markdownDescription: Timeout (seconds) per DMV query. Increase for large databases.

dataLineageViz.dmvQueriesFile.markdownDescription: Path to a custom DMV queries YAML file. Leave empty for built-in defaults. [Customization guide](https://github.com/ChrisDevRepo/vscode_data_lineage/blob/main/docs/DMV_QUERIES.md)

dataLineageViz.tableStatistics.enabled.markdownDescription: Show column statistics and row counts in the table design viewer (database import only).

dataLineageViz.tableStatistics.standardM.markdownDescription: Allow **Standard** profiling mode (adds AVG, STDEV, zero/empty counts). When `false`, only Quick mode is available — lighter queries.

dataLineageViz.tableStatistics.excludeEx.markdownDescription: Skip profiling for external tables. External tables query remote data sources (S3, Blob, other databases) — profiling can be slow and expensive.

dataLineageViz.tableStatistics.sampleThr.markdownDescription: Advanced: Tables above this row count use sampling instead of a full scan. Set to `0` to always sample.

dataLineageViz.tableStatistics.sampleSiz.markdownDescription: Advanced: Number of rows to sample when profiling large tables.

dataLineageViz.tableStatistics.useApprox.markdownDescription: Advanced: Use `APPROX_COUNT_DISTINCT` instead of exact `COUNT(DISTINCT)`. Much faster with ~2% margin. Requires SQL Server 2019+.

dataLineageViz.tableStatistics.maxColumn.markdownDescription: Advanced: Maximum number of columns to profile per table. Columns beyond this cap are skipped. Prevents massive queries on wide tables.

dataLineageViz.layout.direction.markdownDescription: Direction of the dependency graph layout.

enumDescriptions: Smooth step edges with rounded corners

dataLineageViz.layout.edgeAnimation.markdownDescription: Animate edges when running a trace.

dataLineageViz.layout.highlightAnimation.markdownDescription: Animate edges when clicking a node.

dataLineageViz.layout.minimapEnabled.markdownDescription: Show the graph minimap for navigation.

dataLineageViz.layout.rankSeparation.markdownDescription: Advanced: Horizontal spacing between dependency layers (pixels).

dataLineageViz.layout.nodeSeparation.markdownDescription: Advanced: Vertical spacing between nodes in the same layer (pixels).

dataLineageViz.trace.defaultUpstreamLeve.markdownDescription: How many levels upstream (inputs) to trace by default.

dataLineageViz.trace.defaultDownstreamLe.markdownDescription: How many levels downstream (outputs) to trace by default.

dataLineageViz.analysis.hubMinDegree.markdownDescription: Minimum number of connections for an object to appear in the hub list.

dataLineageViz.analysis.islandMaxSize.markdownDescription: Max connected component size to qualify as an island. Default (500) matches `maxNodes` — shows all disconnected subgraphs. Set lower (e.g. 5) to focus on small isolated pairs/groups only.

dataLineageViz.analysis.longestPathMinNo.markdownDescription: Minimum number of objects in a dependency chain for longest-path analysis.

dataLineageViz.ai.enabled.description: Enable the @lineage chat participant and AI lineage tools. Disable for users who do not use VS Code Copilot or want to remove AI features entirely.

dataLineageViz.ai.maxRounds.markdownDescription: Maximum tool-call rounds per `@lineage` request. Increase for complex explorations on deep graphs. Each round is one AI reasoning step + tool call.

dataLineageViz.ai.inlineTokenBudget.markdownDescription: Token budget for inline delivery. When estimated scope tokens are below this threshold, the AI delivers all data in one shot. Lower values force multi-hop state machine exploration. Default: `20000`.

dataLineageViz.ai.outputTemplateFile.markdownDescription: Path to custom AI output templates YAML. Controls how `@lineage` formats summary, description, badges, highlights, and notes. Leave empty for built-in defaults. Use Command Palette → *Create AI Output Templates* to scaffold. [Customization guide](https://github.com/ChrisDevRepo/vscode_data_lineage/blob/main/docs/AI_PROMPTS.md)

chatParticipants.name: lineage
chatParticipants.description: Ask questions about your loaded lineage graph — tables, procedures, dependencies, data flow.

commands.name: trace
commands.description: Trace data lineage for an object or column

commands.name: search
commands.description: Search for database objects by name or schema

disambiguation.description: Questions about database dependencies, data flow, stored procedures, table lineage, SQL object relationships, where data comes from or goes to
examples: Trace the data flow for orders
What tables does this procedure read from
Show me the lineage for the financehub schema
What are the upstream sources of this table

languageModelTools.name: lineage_get_context
languageModelTools.userDescription: Shows what is currently loaded and visible in the lineage graph.
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Returns schemas, stats, active filter, saved views. If objects[] present: full dataset with DDL, columns, FKs, edges — answer directly. Otherwise: use schema names in other tools' schemas[] filters.

languageModelTools.name: lineage_search_objects
languageModelTools.userDescription: Search for database objects by name.
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Search objects by name or column name (substring or mode='regex' for multi-pattern). Returns IDs for BFS/detail. Results with match='column' matched a column inside the object, not the object name. Use schemas[] to narrow scope. For DDL body content search, use search_ddl instead. If 0 results: try search_ddl for DDL body matches before telling the user nothing was found. If 0 results with schemas[]: check schema_mismatch field — object may exist in another schema.

types.description: Include only these object types. Omit for all.

schemas.description: Include only these schemas. Omit for all.

languageModelTools.name: lineage_get_object_detail
languageModelTools.userDescription: Get full details for a specific database object.
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Full metadata for ONE object: columns, FKs, neighbors (up/dn), DDL. Use for single-object questions ('what does spX do?'). For multi-object lineage, use run_bfs_trace instead.

languageModelTools.name: lineage_run_bfs_trace
languageModelTools.userDescription: Trace data lineage upstream and/or downstream from an object.
languageModelTools.modelDescription:
Scope discovery tool — use to understand graph size and shape before deciding on approach. Two modes:
- Level mode (default): id + upstream_hops + downstream_hops → explore by depth
- Path mode: id + target → all nodes on paths between start and end
Returns nodes with DDL, columns, and edges. The result graph is automatically stored — use enrich_view to annotate it. Use prune_node_ids in enrich_view to remove irrelevant nodes. Use schemas[] on large models.
For traced lineage with pruning, use start_column_trace instead (runs token gate, prunes dead ends).

id.description: Exact object ID to trace from

types.description: Include only these node types. Omit for all.

schemas.description: Include only nodes in these schemas. Omit for all.

include_ddl.description: Include DDL/columns in results (default true). Set false for structure-only overview.

target.description: Path mode: find all nodes on paths from id to target. When provided, upstream_hops/downstream_hops are ignored.

languageModelTools.name: lineage_run_analysis
languageModelTools.userDescription: Run structural analysis on the loaded lineage graph.
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Graph-wide structural analysis. Use for: 'most connected objects' or 'biggest nodes' (type=hubs), 'isolated tables with no dependencies' (type=orphans), 'deepest dependency chains' or 'longest paths' (type=longest-path), 'circular dependencies' (type=cycles), 'disconnected groups' (type=islands), 'external or cross-database references' (type=external-refs). Returns ranked groups of node IDs with summary.

min_degree.description: Hubs only: minimum degree to qualify as hub. Default from config.

max_size.description: Islands only: maximum component size to include. Default from config.

languageModelTools.name: lineage_search_ddl
languageModelTools.userDescription: Search SQL body scripts for a text pattern.
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Regex search across SP/view/function DDL bodies. Returns matching lines with context. Use for pattern-based DDL search; for simple keyword name search use search_objects instead.

query.description: Text or regex to search in DDL body scripts (max 200 chars)

types.description: Limit search to these object types. Default: all DDL-bearing types.

languageModelTools.name: lineage_enrich_view
languageModelTools.userDescription: Enrich a stored result graph with a name, summary, optional description, highlight groups, badges, and text annotations. The graph comes from a prior trace, BFS, or exploration — you provide the presentation layer.
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Enrich the stored result graph from your last trace/BFS/exploration. The node set is already stored — you provide presentation: name (mandatory), summary (mandatory), optional description/badges/notes/highlight_groups. Use prune_node_ids to remove irrelevant nodes. Only provide node_ids if you haven't run any trace or BFS (discovery-only mode). Fields are a layered hierarchy: badges (5-8 key nodes, numbered) are navigation anchors; notes caption each badged node; description references step numbers under ## headings with business logic. BAD: 14 badges on all nodes, essay description. GOOD: 6 badges on key nodes, description with '## FX Conversion (step 4)\n`spConvertFX` reads DimRate.Rate…'.

name.description: Display name for the view (max 60 chars)

summary.description: One-line graph purpose for the info card (~120 chars, max 300)

description.description: Optional structured answer — markdown with headings, formulas, column mappings, business logic.

prune_node_ids.description: Node IDs to remove from the stored result graph. Use to prune irrelevant nodes.

node_ids.description: Fallback only — exact node IDs when no stored result graph exists (discovery-only mode). Ignored when a stored graph is available.

layout_direction.description: Graph layout direction — LR for sequential flows, TB for hierarchies (default TB)

highlight_groups.description: Glow on 2-3 critical nodes only. Pick ONE scheme: Lineage (source/transform/target) or Diagnostic (good/warn/fail).

color.description: Semantic role. Lineage: source (blue), transform (orange), target (green). Diagnostic: good (green), warn (yellow), fail (red).

node_ids.description: Node IDs in this group (must reference nodes in the stored result graph)

badges.description: Plain text chips on nodes — label with step number and role (max 50)

notes.description: Annotations below nodes. First line = visible title; rest shown on hover (max 50). Use \n for line breaks.

text.description: First line = title below node. After \n = detail on hover (max 400 chars)

languageModelTools.name: lineage_get_ddl_batch
languageModelTools.userDescription: Get DDL body scripts for multiple objects in one call.
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Batch DDL fetch for known IDs. Use ONLY for objects not already in a BFS result — BFS includes DDL by default. Returns DDL for scriptable objects, type-only for tables.

ids.description: Exact object IDs to fetch DDL for (max 20). Use IDs from lineage_run_bfs_trace or lineage_search_objects.

languageModelTools.name: lineage_start_column_trace
languageModelTools.userDescription: Start a column-level lineage trace through the dependency graph.
languageModelTools.modelDescription:
Start a hop-by-hop lineage trace. Call as first action — origin is optional.

Returns: scope preview + first hop context with focus node DDL + neighbor list.

After receiving: read DDL, verdict each neighbor:
- trace: follow this path. Provide columns_to_trace (INPUT columns) + question.
- prune: cut branch and descendants. Explain why.
- pass: data passes through unchanged, children queued automatically.
Then call lineage_submit_hop_analysis. Repeat until all paths exhausted.

columns_to_trace must be INPUT columns:
  SP computes ListPrice = CostPrice * (1+MarkupPct) → [CostPrice, MarkupPct]
If no columns provided (concept-level trace): omit columns_to_trace from verdicts.

direction.description: Trace direction: up=find sources, down=find consumers

origin.description: Starting object ID. Optional — omit if unknown, the tool will auto-discover.

languageModelTools.name: lineage_submit_hop_analysis
languageModelTools.userDescription: Submit column trace analysis for the current hop.
languageModelTools.modelDescription:
Submit your analysis of the current hop and receive the next hop's data.

Input:
- focus_node_id: extract focus_node.id from the hop context (e.g. hop has focus_node: {id: "[dbo].[X]", ...} → send focus_node_id: "[dbo].[X]")
- notes: free-form text — your findings for this focus node (what you observed in the DDL)
- verdicts: array — per neighbor:
  - neighbor_id: the id field from each neighbor in the neighbors list
  - verdict: trace | prune | pass
  - columns_to_trace: for trace — the INPUT column names to trace upstream (not the output column name)
  - summary: one-line description of what happens at this node
  - question: for trace — what to look for at this neighbor (specific, not vague)

Verdicts:
- trace: follow this path — neighbor carries the traced column or is relevant to the analysis
- prune: cut this branch — neighbor and all descendants removed from the graph
- pass: data passes through unchanged (staging table, simple view) — children queued automatically
- revisit: re-expand a previously pruned node (listed in revisitable field, max 3 per trace)

IMPORTANT — columns_to_trace must be INPUT columns:
  BAD: SP computes ListPrice = CostPrice * (1+MarkupPct) → [ListPrice]
  GOOD: → [CostPrice, MarkupPct]

BAD question: "check this node"
GOOD question: "Does vwSalesData transform Amount or pass it through from staging?"

Trace only columns relevant to the user's question. If asked about cost and TotalRevenue = Qty * UnitPrice, trace [UnitPrice] and prune Qty.

Returns: next hop context OR complete result with full chain when all paths exhausted.
If you receive focus_mismatch error: re-read the hop context and extract focus_node.id correctly.
If you receive invalid_columns error: re-read the DDL and correct the column name, then resubmit.

focus_node_id.description: ID of the focus node from the hop context

notes.description: Your findings for the focus node — free-form text describing what you observed in the DDL

neighbor_id.description: Exact neighbor ID from the hop context

columns_to_trace.description: For trace: INPUT column names to trace in upstream sources (not the output column)

summary.description: One-line: what happens to the column at this node

question.description: For trace: what to look for at this neighbor (specific question, not vague)

languageModelTools.name: lineage_start_exploration
languageModelTools.userDescription: Start a broad exploration of database objects for business rules, documentation, or investigations.
languageModelTools.modelDescription:
Start a broad exploration of database objects. Use for questions about business rules, documentation, SQL patterns, or investigations across many objects.

Provide an origin node — the state machine runs BFS to build the exploration map, seeds the agenda, and returns the first hop.

scope_direction controls what BFS finds — ALWAYS specify it:
- 'upstream': "what are the sources / what feeds into X?" — reverse BFS, finds ancestors only (inbound edges). Use for tables, views.
- 'downstream': "what does X affect / what reads from X?" — forward BFS, finds descendants only (outbound edges). Use for impact analysis.
- 'bidirectional': full connected context — all edges. Only suitable for SPs/functions or when graph is small. Tables/views with high degree will REJECT without explicit direction.
If you omit scope_direction on a highly-connected node the SM will return scope_too_broad and require you to resubmit with a direction. The user's active schema filter is applied as a BFS boundary — exploration stays within filtered schemas.

Returns: scope map (nodes + edges) + first hop context with focus node DDL + neighbors.

After receiving: analyze the focus node DDL/columns. Record your findings:
- findings: detailed analysis (~500 chars) — stored permanently in long-term memory
- summary: one-line digest (~100 chars) — shown in working memory for all future hops
- questions: sub-questions for neighbors you want to investigate (boosts their agenda priority)
- prune_ids: neighbor node IDs to prune (utility functions, unrelated objects). Cascades downstream.

The state machine manages your memory:
- Long-term: full findings stored per node (never lost)
- Working: ALL summaries + ALL pending questions returned each hop
- Checklist: noted/total/open counts

current_task: your own question from a previous hop — answer it.

Do NOT use for column-level tracing — use start_column_trace instead.

origin.description: Starting node ID. SM runs BFS from here to build the exploration map. Use search_objects to find.

scope_direction.description: BFS direction. 'upstream': find all ancestors/sources (inbound edges — what feeds X). 'downstream': find all descendants/consumers (outbound edges — what X affects). 'bidirectional': full connected component (all edges, only for SPs or small graphs). Required for tables/views with high degree.

languageModelTools.name: lineage_submit_findings
languageModelTools.userDescription: Submit analysis of the current node during exploration and receive the next node's data.
languageModelTools.modelDescription:
Submit your analysis of the current exploration hop and receive the next node's data.

Provide:
- verdict (required): 'relevant' = useful findings, 'noted' = acknowledged but not critical, 'irrelevant' = unrelated to the question (all downstream nodes automatically pruned from exploration)
- findings: detailed analysis (~500-3000 chars) — stored permanently in memory
- summary: one-line digest (~100 chars) — shown in working memory for ALL future hops
- questions: sub-questions for nodes to investigate next (boosts their agenda priority)
- prune_ids: neighbor node IDs to remove from agenda. ONLY for neighbors with scope=in_scope — other scopes rejected. Each prune cascades — the node and all nodes only reachable through it are removed. Diamond-safe: fork nodes stay. NOTE: direct neighbors of origin cannot be cascade-pruned.
- add_ids: neighbor node IDs to add to exploration agenda. For neighbors with scope=available that are relevant to your investigation. The node is auto-expanded into scope and queued at high priority.
- complete: set true when you have enough findings to answer the user's question. The SM validates this before accepting: if any direct neighbor of the origin node is unvisited, complete is REJECTED and those nodes are reinjected as mandatory. Check working_memory.remaining_agenda to see what is still open — if it contains SPs or source tables directly connected to the origin, do NOT set complete:true.

If response contains complete_rejected.nodes — those direct neighbors MUST be visited or declared irrelevant before complete:true will be accepted. Do not repeat complete:true until you have processed all of them.

The cascade_if_irrelevant field in the hop context shows how many nodes would be pruned if you mark this node irrelevant. Check it before deciding.

BAD findings: "This SP does stuff with data"
GOOD findings: "Joins Orders with OrderDetails on OrderID, applies WHERE Status='Active', computes TotalAmount = Qty * UnitPrice"
BAD summary: "spBuildSalesReport"
GOOD summary: "Aggregates active orders into revenue totals by region"

Response includes: next hop context OR complete result when agenda is empty OR early_complete result when complete=true.

Working memory in each response shows:
- user_question: your original investigation question (goal anchor — stay focused)
- all_summaries: ALL your one-line summaries from every noted node (never truncated)
- pending_questions: ALL open sub-questions (your checklist)
- remaining_agenda: nodes still to visit — check this BEFORE setting complete:true. If SPs or source tables appear here, you are not done.
- checklist: { noted, total, open, coveragePct }

current_task: your own question for the new focus node — answer it.

focus_node_id.description: ID of the focus node from the hop context

findings.description: Detailed analysis — business rules, transforms, patterns, column flows you observed (~500 chars)

summary.description: One-line digest of your findings, shown in working memory for all future hops (~100 chars)

tags.description: Categorization: business-rule, transform, source, staging, pattern, join, filter, etc.

question.description: What to look for at this node (~150 chars)

questions.description: Sub-questions for nodes to investigate next (boosts their agenda priority)

verdict.description: Your assessment: 'relevant' = useful findings, 'noted' = acknowledged, 'irrelevant' = unrelated (triggers cascade prune of downstream nodes)

prune_ids.description: Neighbor node IDs to prune from exploration. Each pruned node + all nodes only reachable through it are cascade-removed from the agenda.

add_ids.description: Neighbor node IDs to add to exploration agenda. Use for scope=available neighbors that are relevant. Each node is auto-expanded into scope.

complete.description: Set true when you have enough findings to answer the user's question. Triggers early completion — your current finding is processed, then the full result (notes, graph, stats) is returned.

badge_label.description: Short step label for the enriched view badge (2-4 words), e.g. '4 INIT' or '7 Rate Impute'. The result includes suggested_badges built from these labels.

note_caption.description: One-line caption for the enriched view note — what this node does in this flow. e.g. 'Entry point — TRUNCATEs and reloads from staging'. The result includes suggested_notes built from these captions.

scripts.vscode:prepublish: npm run build:ext && npm run build:webview
scripts.build: npm run build:ext && npm run build:webview
scripts.test: tsx test/dacpacExtractor.test.ts && tsx test/graphBuilder.test.ts && tsx test/parser-edge-cases.test.ts && tsx test/graphAnalysis.test.ts && tsx test/dmvExtractor.test.ts && tsx test/tsql-complex.test.ts && tsx test/projectStore.test.ts && tsx test/ai-tools.test.ts && tsx test/column-trace-state.test.ts && tsx test/blackboard-state.test.ts && tsx test/chat-loop.test.ts && tsx test/snapshot-aw-baseline.ts && vitest run --config vitest.config.ts
